name: Wilton
coordsSource: web-verified:GENUKI SU096313;Wikipedia grid SU096312; cross-checked listing-centroid (n=20, gap~0.25km); 2026-06-04
overview: Wilton is a historic market town about three miles west of Salisbury, often described as the ancient capital of Wessex with a history spanning more than 2,000 years. It is best known for Wilton House, seat of the Earls of Pembroke since the 1540s, and for its carpet-making heritage. The town has a market square with a Thursday market and the imposing Italianate church of St Mary and St Nicholas.
character: A thriving small market town with an active community and independent high street, blending working-town practicality with notable heritage on Salisbury's doorstep.
amenities: Thursday market in the Market Square
Independent butchers, bakers, hardware store and cafes
Free short-stay parking (Market Square and South Street)
St Mary and St Nicholas Church and Baptist Church

schools.name: Secondary schools in Salisbury (e.g. Bishop Wordsworth's, South Wilts Grammar, Sarum Academy)

commutes.notes: No station in Wilton; nearest is Salisbury (~4 km), then direct services to London Waterloo

prices.summary: A mix of period town houses, estate cottages and modern developments including the Our Wilton veterans/retirement scheme. More affordable than premium Nadder Valley villages while keeping town amenities. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Wilton House and grounds (grounds, adventure playground and cafe open during 2026 heating refurbishment)
Wilton Town Trail past historic landmarks
St Mary and St Nicholas Church
Events such as Wessex Country Fair and horse trials at Wilton House
placesToEat: Independent cafes around the Market Square
pros: Genuine market-town amenities within minutes of Salisbury
Rich heritage with Wilton House and historic church
Good superfast broadband (median ~80 Mbps in central postcodes)
More affordable than premium outlying villages
cons: No railway station of its own; rely on Salisbury
Sits on busy A36/A30 routes with associated traffic
Less rural feel than smaller surrounding villages
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting town amenities and heritage with quick access to Salisbury, without paying a premium-village price.
primarySupermarket: Local convenience stores in town; larger supermarkets in Salisbury

sources.label: GetTheData - SP2 0DF broadband and postcode stats
